title: How You Actually Spend Your Life

beats.narration: One number should stop you cold — 26 years of your life, gone to sleep.
beats.understand: a shocking number to open — 26 years asleep

flat.label: in one lifetime, you will spend

beats.narration: So where does the rest go? Thirteen years at work. Eleven — more than a decade — lost to a screen. Eight more to eating and chores. Add growing up, and almost none of it is left.
beats.understand: the staged breakdown — a whole lifetime of boxes filling category by category, revealing how little is left

beats.narration: Put it side by side. Eleven years on a screen. Only nine that are truly yours.
beats.understand: the gut-punch — screen time out-weighs your free time, shown as two bars

flat.title: screen time vs your own time

beats.narration: Only about nine years are actually yours. Nine. Spend them on something that matters.

flat.statement: Nine years are yours. Spend them well.
